Serving All of Hays County, TX

Hays County Stucco Repair & Installation

Hays County is one of the fastest-growing regions in Texas, and new construction alongside established neighborhoods means stucco needs range from fresh installations on custom builds to urgent repairs on homes showing their age. Star Stucco of Austin has completed more than 600 projects across the county, and we bring that depth of experience to every job in Kyle, Buda, San Marcos, Dripping Springs, and beyond.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Understanding Stucco Challenges in Hays County

Rapid development across Hays County has transformed once-rural land into thriving neighborhoods, but growth brings its own set of challenges for exterior finishes. Homes built quickly during boom periods sometimes have stucco applied over inadequately prepared substrates, leading to premature cracking and water infiltration within just a few years of construction.

The county sits along the Balcones Escarpment where expansive black clay soils dominate the eastern half and rocky limestone underlies the western hills. This geological split means foundation movement patterns differ dramatically from Kyle to Wimberley, and stucco damage follows suit. We tailor our approach based on which part of the county your home occupies.

Seasonal thunderstorms push heavy rain against south- and east-facing walls, and the combination of wind-driven water and aging caulk joints creates intrusion points that go unnoticed until interior damage appears. Our inspections focus on these vulnerable areas first, catching problems while they are still manageable.

Stucco Services We Offer in Hays County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Hays County.

Stucco Repair

Hays County homes develop stucco damage from foundation shifts on expansive clay and rapid temperature changes. We assess every repair holistically, fixing the root cause alongside the visible damage so the problem does not return next season.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

With hundreds of custom homes going up in Dripping Springs, Wimberley, and the corridor between Kyle and Buda, demand for quality stucco installation is high. We work directly with builders and homeowners to deliver finishes that meet design goals and perform for decades.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Black clay soils in eastern Hays County swell dramatically when wet and shrink during droughts, pulling foundations and cracking stucco walls. We use reinforced patching systems and flexible sealants designed to accommodate ongoing soil movement.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Water intrusion behind stucco is the most common call we receive from Hays County homeowners. Storm-driven rain finds its way through failed sealant joints, and once inside the wall, it rots sheathing and breeds mold. We remove damaged material and rebuild with proper drainage layers.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Custom homes in the Dripping Springs and Driftwood areas often feature hand-troweled plaster exteriors that need skilled maintenance. We replicate original textures and color mixes so touch-ups and re-coats integrate perfectly with the existing facade.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

EIFS is popular on newer Hays County developments for its energy efficiency and design flexibility. We install these systems with drainage planes that handle the county's heavy spring rains, and we repair existing EIFS that has been compromised by moisture or impact.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

Retail centers, office parks, and restaurants along the I-35 corridor through Kyle, Buda, and San Marcos depend on attractive stucco exteriors. We handle large-scale commercial projects on tight schedules, coordinating with general contractors and property managers.

Learn More

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Hays County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Hays County

The combination of rapid growth, expansive soils, and severe weather makes Hays County a hotspot for stucco issues. These are the problems we diagnose and fix most often across the area.

Cracks

Foundation movement on Hays County clay soils is the leading cause of stucco cracks in the area. Horizontal and stair-step cracks often appear within a few years of construction, especially on homes built during periods of drought followed by heavy rain.

Bubbling or soft stucco

When water gets behind stucco through failed flashings or missing kick-out diverters, the base coat absorbs moisture and loses its bond. We see this frequently on two-story homes in Kyle and Buda where upper-floor roof transitions were not properly detailed.

Water stains

Dark streaks below windows and along wall bases indicate water is traveling down behind or across the stucco surface. In Hays County, these stains often appear after spring storm season and signal sealant or flashing failures that need prompt repair.

Window and door leaks

Newer construction in Hays County sometimes relies on minimal caulking around window flanges instead of integrated flashing membranes. Over time this caulk shrinks and cracks, allowing wind-driven rain to enter the wall and cause interior water damage.

Failing patches

Homeowners or handymen who attempt quick stucco patches without proper bonding agents or compatible mixes create repairs that peel away within months. We remove these failed patches and redo the work correctly so it blends and lasts.

Separation around trim

As Hays County homes go through seasonal temperature swings, the junction between stucco and foam or wood trim pulls apart. These gaps collect rainwater and funnel it into the wall cavity, making trim separation a priority repair item.

Impact damage

Hailstorms crossing Hays County leave pockmarks and chips in stucco finishes, particularly on west-facing walls. Lawn equipment and accidental contact also cause impact damage at ground level that exposes the base coat to moisture.

Faded or stained exterior walls

South-facing stucco walls in Hays County take the brunt of Texas sun and lose their original color within several years. Algae and mildew also develop on shaded walls, leaving green or black discoloration that diminishes curb appeal.

How We Work in Hays County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule inspection

Reach out to us by phone, email, or our online form. We cover all of Hays County and can typically schedule your inspection within a few business days, often sooner.

2

Review scope

We perform a thorough on-site evaluation, testing suspect areas with moisture meters and documenting damage with photos. You get a clear explanation of what needs repair and why, with no technical jargon.

3

Get estimate

Within 24 hours of the inspection, you receive a detailed estimate outlining every line item. We answer questions and adjust scope as needed before any work begins.

4

Complete work

Our crew handles the project from start to finish, keeping you informed at each stage. We clean up daily, complete a final walkthrough with you, and make sure you are satisfied before closing out the job.
